rulings.Case Title: RAFIK EL-HANI et al VS. FEDEX GROUND PACKAGING, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively, Summ Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 7, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANTs FEDEX GROUND PACKAGE SYSTEM, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication. The Court grants the motion for summary judgment. Plaintiffs cannot establish the third cause of action for breach of contract/guarantee. The contract is too uncertain and the statute of frauds bars the claim. Plaintiff Rafik El-Hani's declaration, which states that defendant guaranteed $250,000, contradicts his deposition testimony and does not create a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiffs failed to show that defendant had a personal, immediate, and pecuniary interest in the transaction and therefore, the exception to the statute of frauds does not apply. Plaintiffs cannot establish the fourth cause of action for violation of the UCL because plaintiffs had an adequate remedy at law, plaintiffs cannot obtain an injunction because it is unlikely that the alleged practice will occur again, and defendant has nothing to restore to plaintiffs. Plaintiffs cannot establish the sixth and seventh causes of action for concealment and fraud because there is no evidence of scienter and plaintiffs were aware that the termination package needed to be forwarded to corporate headquarters. Plaintiffs cannot establish the extortion cause of action because defendant acted within its contractual rights, i.e., there was no duress, and defendant never received any property from plaintiff. Plaintiff cannot establish the Unruh Act claim because it is undisputed that the decision makers did not express any discriminatory animus. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LISA HABBESHAW VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 7, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (PART 1 OF 2). Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is denied. Defendant met its burden of establishing the defect was "trivial" under Govt. Code sec. 830(a). (Undisputed Fact ("UF") no. 7; Decl. of Mike Haase, 3, Ex. A.) The burden then shifted to Plaintiff to show a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiff met her burden by producing a zoomed-in photograph of the raised brick, showing pieces were broken off and jagged edges were present. (Plantiff's Evidence, Ex. 2H.) Jagged breaks are aggravating factors that militate against a finding that a condition is trivial as a matter of law. (Fielder v. City of Glendale ((1977) 71 Cal.App.3d 719, 726.) Therefore, Plaintiff met her burden establishing there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether the jagged pieces of raised bricks were trivial or a dangerous condition. To prevail on her sole cause of action for dangerous condition of public property, Plaintiff also has to establish that Defendant had actual or constructive notice of the dangerous condition. (Govt. Code sec. 835.) Defendant met its burden of establishing it did not have actual or constructive notice of the jagged pieces of the raised bricks. (UF nos. 12, 15, 16.) The burden again shifted to Plaintiff to show a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiff did not meet her burden in producing competent evidence showing there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendant had actual or constructive notice. Plaintiff presented no evidence about when the dangerous condition, the jagged break of the raised bricks, first arose. (tentative ruling continues, see part 2) =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LISA HABBESHAW VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment (Part 2 Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 7, 2014, Line 5, Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment (PART 2 OF 2). The Declaration of Jeff Mastin, Plaintiff's expert, lacks foundation (Evid. Code sec. 403), is an unqualified expert opinion (Evid. Code sec. 720), and is based on improper matter (Evid. Code sec. 804) as to when the breaking of the bricks occurred. "[I]n determining constructive notice, the method of inspection is secondary; the primary and indispensable element of constructive notice is a showing that the condition existed before the accident¿[there must be sufficient evidence to show whether] the condition has existed long enough that it may be inferred that a reasonable inspection would have ascertained its existence." (Strongman v. County of Kern (1967) 255 Cal.App.2d 308, 313.) Further, Plaintiff has not met her burden of showing actual notice because Plaintiff presented no evidence establishing that a public officer had knowledge of the jagged break of the raised bricks, given that there is no evidence whether the jagged break was present in 2008 or 2009. (See State v. Superior Court for San Mateo County (1968) 263 Cal.App.2d 396, 399.) Plaintiff's request to continue the hearing date pursuant to CCP sec. 437c(h) is denied. The motion was originally filed on July 2, 2013. Plaintiff has had three requests to continue the hearing date granted. Plaintiff had ample time to file a motion to compel depositions but failed to do so. rulings.Case Title: MT. TAM LASER AND SKIN CARE CORPORATION et al VS. RONALD P. GOLDMAN, A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents By Dr. Wolf In Response To Request For Production No. 10 And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 7, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS MT. TAM LASER AND SKIN CARE CORPORATION, KAROL MEIER AND SANDI SELIG-FARNEY'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents By Dr. Wolf In Response To Request For Production No. 10 And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED. The responsive documents identified are protected by the attorney-client privilege, and Plaintiffs' have failed to show the responsive documents identified are relevant to the instant matter. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is DENIED. Defendant's request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HELGA KATRINA HORNER VS. DARIN GEISE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Deft Coup D'Etat, Inc. To Further Respond To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Numbers 1-32 And 35-48
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 7, 2014, Line 9, PLAINTIFFs HELGA HORNER and JOHN TOBELER's Motion For An Order Compelling Defendant Coup D'Etat, Inc. To Further Respond To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Numbers 1-32 And 35-48. Denied. Each request seeks information pertaining to the alter ego allegations. This information is protected by the financial privacy privilege. Defendants Geise and Coup d'Etat have averred that Plaintiffs' claims are fully covered by Defendants' general liability policy and, under this policy, their insurer has accepted tender of the defense and indemnity in this action. In light of this averment, Defendant Geise's privacy interests currently outweighs Plaintiffs need to conduct discovery in support of their alter ego allegations. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: HELGA KATRINA HORNER VS. DARIN GEISE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Deft Darin Geise Dba Coup D'Etat To Provide Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Numbers 1,2,3,4 And 5; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 7, 2014, Line 9, PLAINTIFFS HELGA HORNER AND JOHN TOBELER'S Motion For An Order Compelling Defendant Darin Geise Dba Coup D'Etat To Provide Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, Numbers 1,2,3,4 And 5; Proof Of Service. Granted as to Request nos. 1, 3 - 5. All general objections to the Request for Production are overruled. Denied as to Request no. 2. For Request nos. 1, 3 - 5, within 10 days of service of notice of entry of order, Defendant Geise shall provide amended verified responses either averring that all responsive documents have been produced or state with particularity which documents have been withheld based on an objection. For any responsive document withheld on the basis of privilege, Defendant Geise shall serve a privilege log that identifies the nature of the document, date, author, recipients, Bates numbers, and the privilege claimed. For Request no. 2, Plaintiffs seek information and documents pertaining to the alter ego allegations. This information is protected by the financial privacy privilege. Defendants Geise and Coup d'Etat have averred that Plaintiffs' claims are fully covered by Defendants' general liability policy and, under this policy, their insurer has accepted tender of the defense and indemnity in this action. In light of this averment, Defendant Geise's privacy interests currently outweighs Plaintiffs need to conduct discovery in support of their alter ego allegations. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: ANDRE GARCIA VS. AXIS CONSTRUCTION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 7, 2014, Line 10, CROSS DEFENDANT HIPPWRAP CONTAINMENT, INC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained. Cross-Defendant's demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first and second causes of action. The facts in the First Amended Cross-Complaint show that both parties did not sign the Subcontract Agreement before the injury to Plaintiff occurred on November 17, 2011. (First Amended Cross-Complaint, 13, 15.) Following Nielsen Construction Company v. International Iron Products (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 863, 869, the express indemnity clause in the Subcontract Agreement is unenforceable under Labor Code § 3864 because both parties did not sign the Subcontract Agreement before the injury occurred. The City of Oakland v. Delcon Associates (1985) 168 Cal.App.3d 1126, case is not controlling because it is factually distinguishable. As to the third cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as Cross-Complainant relies on facts not alleged in the First Amended Cross-Complaint. Cross-Complainant is given 10 days leave to amend to plead facts, if it can do in good faith, regarding Cross-Defendant's failure to obtain an additional insured endorsement in violation of the Subcontract Agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ENVIRONMENTAL RESEARCH CENTER, INC VS. NATURES'S ANSWER, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 7, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT NATURE'S ANSWER, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained without leave to amend pursuant to CCP sec. 430.10(e). The complaint was not filed within the applicable one-year statute of limitations. (CCP sec. 340.) The cause of action accrued on July 18, 2012, the first day Plaintiff had the right to file suit after serving the 60 day notice and certificate of merit pursuant to Health & Saf. Code sec. 25249.7(d)(1). The complaint was not filed until October 11, 2013, and therefore the second cause of action is time-barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ENVIRONMENTAL RESEARCH CENTER, INC VS. NATURES'S ANSWER, INC et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, February 7, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT NATURE'S ANSWER, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Denied as moot, in light of the ruling on demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.
